summary: This week in AI, significant advancements were made in LLMs, with OpenAI launching its new family of models, including GPT-5.6. Apple sued OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft, while Anthropic started localizing Claude pricing for India. The industry also saw the rise of open-source AI, with Hugging Face's CEO emphasizing its importance.
insight: The increasing focus on open-source AI and the launch of new LLMs indicate a shift towards more collaborative and accessible AI development, which could lead to faster innovation and broader adoption.
highlights: OpenAI launches GPT-5.6, promising improvements in cybersecurity and other areas
Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft, highlighting the intense competition in the AI industry
Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US, indicating growing interest in AI adoption globally
